action.content: Write a 30-line poem with short sentences without any comma. Each line should contain exactly one sentence. Make sure that you put the right punctuation at the end of each line. Your entire response should contain the poem only.
action.label: context

action.content:
The sun rises high above the hill.
Birds sing songs of morning light.
The wind blows through the tall trees.
Clouds drift slowly across the sky.
A cat walks across the warm floor.
The dog barks at a passing car.
Rain falls softly on the roof.
The river flows toward the sea.
Flowers bloom in the garden bed.
Children play in the dusty street.
The clock strikes twelve at night.
Stars appear in the dark blue sky.
The moon shines bright and cold.
Shadows stretch across the ground.
A bird lands on the fence post.
The leaves turn colors in the air.
Time moves forward without a sound.
We watch the world go by so fast.
Hope remains inside our hearts today.
Tomorrow brings new things to see.
The day ends with a quiet sigh.
Rest comes after all the work is done.
Dreams float away into the night.
Sleep takes hold of tired eyes.
The world waits for the next sunrise.
Life continues in every breath we take.
Peace fills the space between the stars.
This is the end of our short poem.
action.label: response
